title: Malaysian Chicken Curry

subs.name: 700 g chickpeas (tinned or boiled) for a vegetarian Malaysian curry
150 g coconut milk powder dissolved in 300 ml warm water
1 tsp lemon zest or 2 tbsp lemon juice

ingredients.name: kaffir lime leaves
visual_checks: tear the leaves before adding; substitute with lime zest
ingredients.notes: tear the leaves before adding; substitute with lime zest

subs.name: zest of 1 lime stirred in at the end

ingredients.name: red chilli powder (use Kashmiri for colour)
curry powder
visual_checks: Malaysian curry powder or any mild chicken masala blend
ingredients.notes: Malaysian curry powder or any mild chicken masala blend

ingredients.name: potato, peeled and quartered
visual_checks: traditional addition that makes the dish go further
ingredients.notes: traditional addition that makes the dish go further

steps.text: Blend the chopped onion into a smooth paste. Heat oil in a kadai on medium heat; add lemongrass and torn kaffir lime leaves and fry for 30 seconds until aromatic.
Add the onion paste and fry, stirring, for 6–8 minutes until it turns golden and the raw smell disappears.
Add ginger-garlic paste and stir for 2 minutes until fragrant. Add all the dry spices — turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder and curry powder — and fry for 1 minute on low heat.
Add the chicken pieces and turn the heat to high. Sear for 4–5 minutes, stirring, until the chicken is coated in the spice paste and has some colour on the outside.
Add potato pieces if using. Pour in 200 ml warm water and salt. Cover and simmer on medium heat for 15 minutes.

Pour in the coconut milk and stir gently. Simmer uncovered on medium-low for 10–12 minutes until the gravy thickens slightly and the chicken is cooked through — the juices should run clear when pierced. Remove lemongrass before serving.
Taste for salt and heat. The curry should be a rich, golden-orange colour with a thin, coconut-fragrant gravy. Serve with steamed rice, roti canai or bread.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → 700 g chickpeas (tinned or boiled) for a vegetarian Malaysian curry (skip the initial searing step); coconut-milk → 150 g coconut milk powder dissolved in 300 ml warm water (shelf-stable and widely available); kaffir-lime-leaves → zest of 1 lime stirred in at the end; lemongrass → 1 tsp lemon zest or 2 tbsp lemon juice
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This dish is very close to Indian-style coconut chicken curry and adapts naturally to a kadai. Use any good chicken masala or a mild curry powder blend; Kashmiri chilli gives the golden-orange colour without excessive heat. Lemongrass is now stocked in many Indian supermarkets; substitute with a strip of lemon zest if unavailable. Coconut milk tetra packs are widely available across India.
